pr.title: Publish packages for building Melee and generating documentation
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Unmerged tooling/CI PR that proposed replacing the existing build and publish workflows with GHCR-hosted container images for Linux builds, Windows smoke-test builds, and documentation generation. It added Dockerfiles and entrypoints under .github/packages, split build-melee, publish-pages, and publish-packages workflows, and moved CI setup work into reusable images with dry-run validation before publishing. The PR was closed because it was superseded by #752; author noted current builds failed because the new images did not yet exist under doldecomp.
what_changed: Added a new publish-packages GitHub Actions workflow to build GHCR images for build-linux, gen-pages, and build-windows, with scheduled weekly runs, path-based triggers, concurrency cancellation, and Docker metadata tags such as latest, edge, branch refs, PR refs, sha tags, and scheduled date tags.

Replaced the old .github/workflows/build.yml with a new build-melee.yml that pulls prebuilt GHCR images from the current GitHub repository and runs GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1 as separate matrix entries with fail-fast disabled.

Added a Linux build container based on ubuntu:latest that copies in WiBo from ghcr.io/decompals/wibo:latest, copies powerpc-eabi-as from devkitpro/devkitppc:latest, downloads the MELEE_COMPILERS.zip archive, sets WINE/DEVKITPRO/DEVKITPPC/PATH, and runs make through an entrypoint.

Added a Windows Server Core 2022 container intended primarily as a smoke test for Windows developer setup. It uses PowerShell 5.1, creates an unprivileged melee user, installs git/python/mingw through scoop, creates a Python venv, installs requirements.txt, downloads compilers, and builds through bash because the Makefile does not support cmd or PowerShell directly.

Added a gen-pages container that installs doxygen, Python, requirements.txt, clones doxygen-awesome-css, runs doxygen, creates .nojekyll, runs tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py -pqr, and copies ctx.html into the generated documentation output.

Changed documentation publishing to use the prebuilt page generator image, generate pages for pull requests as a smoke test, and upload generated pages as an artifact while only publishing GitHub Pages from master or main.
Added .dockerignore entries for .git directories, __pycache__, venv, and .idea to reduce Docker build context noise.

Added requirements.txt files under tools/calcprogress and tools/calcprogress/src, likely to support Python package installation or packaging behavior, though the excerpt only shows one-line additions without details.
Made build artifacts and progress more container-oriented: Linux entrypoint copies build outputs to /output and creates build/PROGRESS.md from calcprogress when GALE01.map exists.
smart_moves: Moved expensive and fragile dependency setup out of every CI run and into prebuilt images, making normal build-melee runs depend on the latest successful image rather than live upstream availability.

Used dry runs before publishing each image: build images try both GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1, while the page image tries documentation generation. This prevents broken images from becoming the latest GHCR package.
Avoided hardcoding doldecomp in the consuming workflows by deriving image names from github.repository, allowing forks to build and consume their own package namespace.
Kept GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1 as separate matrix jobs with fail-fast disabled so one build mode can fail without suppressing evidence from the other.
Used GHCR tag strategy with latest, edge, branch, PR, sha, and scheduled date tags, giving both convenient defaults and reproducible image references.
For Windows, deliberately smoke-tested a realistic unprivileged local setup path using PowerShell 5.1 and scoop rather than relying only on GitHub runner state.

Explicitly checked $LASTEXITCODE after native commands in PowerShell scripts, acknowledging that PowerShell 5.1 does not automatically fail on native executable errors even with strict mode and ErrorActionPreference.
Synced Python requirements again at container entrypoint time for Windows and gen-pages so images remain usable when requirements.txt changes after the image was built.
Used cached base images from GitHub-hosted runners to make very large Ubuntu and Windows base layers inexpensive to pull in CI.
Preserved developer-facing artifacts: GALE01.map remains uploaded for GENERATE_MAP=1 runs, and generated documentation is uploaded as a zipped artifact even when not published to Pages.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation projects with pinned or unusual compiler toolchains, CI reliability can be improved by publishing project-specific build images that include WiBo, devkitPPC assembler support, and mwcc compiler placement conventions.
Container entrypoints should mirror the repository's expected local layout, such as linking or copying mwcc_compiler into tools/ before invoking make.
When validating matching and non-matching configurations, keep GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1 independent so regressions in one mode do not hide the other mode's status.
Documentation tooling should be smoke-tested in pull requests, because tools such as doxygen, m2ctx.py, Python dependencies, and CSS assets can fail independently of the C build.
Windows CI can serve as a developer-experience test rather than only a build test; using a fresh Server image and unprivileged user exposes setup assumptions that may affect real contributors.

If CI workflows consume newly introduced package images, the bootstrap problem matters: builds can fail until images exist under the target organization. A migration may need a staged merge or fallback strategy.
For fork-friendly automation, avoid hardcoded organization names and use GitHub context variables for registry image paths and repository ownership.
naming_conventions: New workflows were named by purpose with hyphenated names: build-melee, publish-pages, publish-packages.
Container package targets were hyphenated by role: build-linux, build-windows, gen-pages.
Environment variables used uppercase descriptive names such as REGISTRY, IMAGE, IMAGE_NAME, CONTAINERFILE, MAKE_FLAGS, WINE, DEVKITPRO, DEVKITPPC, and MELEE_COMPILERS_URL.
Docker volume mount points were simple role names: /input and /output on Linux containers, C:/Input and C:/Output on Windows containers.
Published image tags followed common container naming semantics: latest, edge, branch ref, PR ref, sha, and scheduled YYYYMMDD date tags.
Build modes remained expressed through existing Makefile flags: GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The Linux container copied powerpc-eabi-as from devkitpro/devkitppc:latest into the expected DEVKITPPC bin path and set DEVKITPRO, DEVKITPPC, and PATH so the Makefile could find assembler tooling.
The Linux build image copied WiBo from ghcr.io/decompals/wibo:latest and exported WINE to the WiBo path, allowing Windows mwcc tooling to run in Linux CI.
The build-linux entrypoint symlinked /opt/mwcc_compiler into tools/ before invoking make, preserving the repository's expected compiler directory convention.
The Windows entrypoint copied the saved mwcc_compiler directory into tools/mwcc_compiler before running make, matching the layout expected by the existing build system.
The Linux GENERATE_MAP=1 path ran tools/calcprogress/calcprogress.py against build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol and GALE01.map with --asm-obj-ext .s.o and --old-map true to generate PROGRESS.md.
The new CI matrix continued to exercise both GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1, preserving coverage of matching/progress and non-matching build configurations.

review_feedback.feedback: Builds were failing because the new images did not yet exist under doldecomp; the author said they would force-merge once approved.
review_feedback.outcome: The PR was not merged and was later closed.

review_feedback.feedback: A note about branch filtering changed twice: first saying there was no point to filtering the branch, then clarifying that the attempted filtering was not allowed.
review_feedback.outcome: No final reviewer-driven resolution is visible in the provided excerpt.

follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #752 to see the merged or revised version of the GHCR package workflow.
Check whether current build-melee workflows still derive image names from github.repository or moved back to doldecomp-specific images.
Look for later changes to the Windows build image, especially whether scoop was replaced by winget or PowerShell 7 behavior.
Search for later fixes to the package bootstrap problem where consuming workflows fail before GHCR images exist.
Review current calcprogress requirements placement to understand why both tools/calcprogress/requirements.txt and tools/calcprogress/src/requirements.txt were added.
